finding: Rerank reorder (slot-take + append-omitted) reviewed for dup/loss/out-of-range — correct: get_mut guards OOB, slot.take() guards double-take, remaining appended; truncated recomputed vs top_k after widen.
Composer attach/detach were fired with `void`, so a rejected attachConversation/detachConversation promise was a silent unhandled rejection with no user feedback.
KbSourcePanel getTextRects effect has a `cancelled` guard + clearTarget on unmount; loadForConversation guards a stale response via currentConversationId compare — no clobber/leak.
get_text_rects is owner-scoped (get_by_id_and_user → 404 on non-owned) and clamps start/end to page_text.len() with char-boundary-safe slice; no IDOR / panic on user input.

resolve_scope_file_ids and attached_kbs_for_* join knowledge_bases WHERE kb.user_id=$1; a KB/file id the caller doesn't own contributes nothing (no cross-user leak). All KB handlers use RequirePermissions with the correct use/manage perm.

Both required mcp.rs edits present (auto_attach_builtin_ids push + is_builtin_server_id approval-bypass); KB docs fns carry .id() + .response() so the generated client is typed; sync entity KnowledgeBase is owner-scoped (Some(user_id)), never everyone().

The KB composer had no conversation-change reset (unlike the file extension's useChatStore.subscribe(conversation?.id)); onConversationLoad only fires for EXISTING conversations, so the pending selectedKbIds from conversation A leaked into a fresh new chat and would be attached to it on first send.

Picker option rows carry role=button + tabIndex + Enter/Space keydown + aria-pressed; the PDF highlight overlay is pointer-events:none and purely decorative (no interactive/text content) so needs no name. No a11y defect found.

Mirrors mcp/literature/citations idioms: SearchKnowledgeToolResultCard carries a static contentMatch (scopes to search_knowledge so literature/file catch-alls still run), KbMenuItem/KbStatusRow match McpMenuItem/McpStatusRow, project-extension matches ProjectBibliography*. No divergence found.

Per-KB indexing_summary is a per-row subquery in a loop (N+1), but this mirrors the existing file_rag list() pattern and KB counts are user-scoped/small; rerank candidate pool is capped at 200 (validated). Acceptable, no change.
The 7 enumerated e2e specs (TEST-37..43) were not yet authored at audit time; the highest-value gaps to cover are cross-user 404, cap-422, empty-hits, no-embedder fallback, and rerank-disabled path.

User-facing strings reviewed for consistency/leakage: 'Knowledge base search', indexing-incomplete banner, untrusted-content note — consistent terminology, no internal leakage, plural handling present. No copy defect.
setHighlights did not clamp the target page to [1, pagesCount]; a stale/off-by-one citation page would leave pdf.js at its current page and the highlight layer would never render.
